title: Artemis 2 heads to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has launched toward the moon following a successful maneuver on April 2. This marks a significant step in the agency's lunar exploration program.

title: Swift spacecraft reorientation buys time for reboost mission
summary: NASA adjusted the Swift spacecraft's operations to extend its time in a decaying orbit, allowing more time for a planned reboost mission later this year. The maneuver aims to raise the spacecraft's orbit before it degrades further.

title: Former Sierra Space CEO Tom Vice to lead Astrion
summary: Tom Vice, former CEO of Sierra Space, has been appointed to lead Astrion, a Huntsville-based defense contractor specializing in systems engineering, integration, and space mission assurance.

title: Optical terminals still a bottleneck in Pentagon’s proliferated constellation
summary: The Pentagon faces challenges with optical communication terminals for its proliferated satellite constellation, as current capabilities are insufficient. Industry experts highlight the need for more advanced and scalable solutions.

title: Pentagon awards Raytheon $45 million for GPS ground system as program future is reassessed
summary: The Pentagon awarded Raytheon a $45 million contract for a GPS ground system to support satellite launches while officials reassess the program's future. The decision comes as the long-delayed ground system faces an uncertain path.

title: Moog Technology Successfully Steers Artemis II Launch
summary: Moog Inc. successfully provided critical technology for steering the Artemis II launch. The company, a designer of precision motion and control systems, played a key role in the mission.

title: Carmel Ortiz on the evolving landscape of satellite communications
summary: Carmel Ortiz, SES's senior vice president of medium-Earth-orbit constellation programs, discusses new satellite communication technologies on Space Minds. The interview highlights advancements in satellite communications within SES's operations.

title: Phantom Space buys thermal specialist to support orbital data center push
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies to support the development of its Phantom Cloud orbital data center constellation. The acquisition aims to enhance thermal management capabilities for the planned constellation.

title: Relativity, Hermeus, Astrion and Divergent executives join Fortastra C-suite
summary: Fortastra, a Los Angeles startup, has hired executives from Relativity Space, Hermeus, Astrion, and Divergent Technologies to lead its C-suite for developing maneuverable spacecraft aimed at on-orbit security. Key hires include Josh Jetter as CTO and Sahil Desai as VP of product.

title: Artemis 2 launches on first human mission to the moon in more than 50 years
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission launched on April 1, marking the first human mission to the moon in over 50 years. The mission involves four astronauts flying around the moon.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts head for the moon after make-or-break engine burn (video)
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts successfully executed a critical engine burn, propelling the Orion capsule from Earth orbit toward the moon. This milestone marks a significant step forward in NASA's lunar mission preparations.

title: Lucky airplane passengers capture NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch from the sky
summary: Airplane passengers captured NASA's Artemis 2 moon rocket launch from the sky, providing unique aerial footage of the event.

title: Watch comet C/2026 A1 plunge toward the sun online this week
summary: Comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS), a rare sungrazing comet, will be visible online as it approaches the sun this week. This event offers a unique opportunity for astronomers and the public to observe its trajectory.

title: 'The universe shall quake in my shadow' — 'Masters of the Universe' looks like the summer's most metal movie
summary: A trailer for the upcoming movie 'Masters of the Universe' (MOTU) has been released, featuring Jared Leto as Skeletor. The trailer showcases a dramatic confrontation between Skeletor and He-Man.

title: 'Supergirl' goes full John Wick to save her dog Krypto in new trailer
summary: The news mentions a trailer for 'Supergirl' where the character saves her dog Krypto in a stylized action sequence. The trailer's tagline emphasizes the bond between Supergirl and her dog.

title: Stunning new James Webb Space Telescope images reveal 'hidden' stars being born
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to capture stunning new images of star formation in the W51 region, revealing previously hidden stars being born.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch seen from space | Space photo of the day for April 2, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission launched on a historic journey around the moon and back, with the moment captured from space. This marks a significant milestone in lunar exploration.

title: How to track the Artemis 2 astronauts on their journey around the moon
summary: NASA has launched a website allowing real-time tracking of the Artemis 2 astronauts during their lunar mission. The mission marks a significant step in human space exploration.

title: Relive NASA's historic Artemis 2 launch to the moon in these stunning photos
summary: NASA's historic Artemis 2 mission launch to the moon is captured in stunning photos. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: Barents Sea Tied to Low Arctic Sea Ice
summary: Patches of open water in the Barents Sea contributed to the lowest Arctic sea ice extent in March 2026, matching the record low observed in satellite data.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Mission Leaves Earth Orbit for Flight around Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully completed a translunar injection burn, propelling the Orion spacecraft and its crew out of Earth's orbit for a flight around the Moon. This marks the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Launch to Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission will launch four astronauts on a 10-day journey around the Moon and back in April 2026. The mission will be conducted using NASA's Space Launch System rocket and Orion spacecraft.

title: Réunion Island Lava Reaches the Sea
summary: The February 2026 eruption of Réunion Island's Piton de la Fournaise volcano has lasted longer and produced more lava than recent eruptions. The lava flow has now reached the sea.

title: Liftoff! NASA Launches Astronauts on Historic Artemis Moon Mission
summary: NASA successfully launched the Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The SLS rocket lifted off from Kennedy Space Center, carrying four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft.

title: March of the Harmattan
summary: In March 2026, strong Harmattan winds carried Saharan dust across northwestern Africa toward the Canary Islands, significantly reducing visibility and prompting weather alerts.

title: Godspeed, Artemis II!
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir shared a photo of the Artemis program patch aboard the International Space Station, highlighting the mission's progress toward returning humans to the Moon. The Artemis II mission is set to launch soon, building on ISS research.

title: Landsat Reveals Reservoir Changes and Bathymetry
summary: Researchers used Landsat data to analyze reservoir changes and bathymetry in two recent studies. This highlights the satellite's role in advancing water resource management and understanding.

title: Sendoff for Artemis II Crew
summary: The Artemis II crew, including NASA astronauts Victor Glover, Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman, and CSA astronauts Jeremy Hansen and Jenni Gibbons, posed for a photo before a media event on March 27, 2026. Douglas and Gibbons are designated as backup crew members.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon. This achievement comes over 50 years after the last crewed lunar mission.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: NASASpaceFlight.com reports that the Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This follows the historic Apollo 17 mission, which concluded on Dec. 16, 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, including SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz rockets, with liftoffs planned from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is highlighted in the context of NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in space exploration. The article emphasizes the role of the rocket factory in supporting NASA's missions beyond low Earth orbit.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the Transporter 16 mission, delivering 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway program. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. The mission was unveiled by Administrator Jared Isaacman and other agency leaders.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to Starbase launch pads in preparation for the next-generation Block 3 Starships, aiming to increase launch frequency. The groundwork highlights the company's focus on enhancing operational efficiency at its Boca Chica facility.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for launches.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website provides industry news related to the space sector. No specific details about the news content are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that can perform multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space electronics. This innovation could enhance the capabilities of satellites and spacecraft.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the successful reconnection.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is set for a January 2027 launch on an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, requiring all suppliers to use it for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna for satellite navigation that offers resilience against interference while being cost-effective and simple to manufacture. This innovation addresses the growing risks of disruption to satellite navigation systems.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the root cause and working to restore communication.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a plan for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed landings starting next year, which has CLPS companies eager to participate. The initiative, dubbed an 'Opportunity Bomb,' aims to accelerate lunar exploration and commercial opportunities.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications. This acquisition aims to enhance Phantom Space's capabilities in satellite thermal management.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with four astronauts, including the first woman and first person of color to head to the Moon, as well as the first non-American astronaut. The historic crew includes NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding to develop an uncrewed space station for hosting manufacturing infrastructure in orbit.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has secured $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with additional investment from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other new investors.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker expressed strong support for establishing a Moon base as a strategic step towards future Mars missions. The comments highlight growing enthusiasm within the space community for lunar exploration as part of the Artemis program.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager and Icarus are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), marking a significant step in space technology development. This partnership highlights the growing role of private companies in advancing ISS capabilities.

title: Q&A With Lunar Base Manager Carlos Garcia-Galan
summary: Carlos Garcia-Galan transitioned from managing NASA's lunar-orbiting habitat program to overseeing the agency's plan for a lunar surface base. He discussed these efforts during NASA HQ's Ignition event.

title: Transporter-16 Sends 119 Payloads to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-16 mission successfully deployed 119 payloads into orbit. This marks another rideshare mission under the company's Smallsat Rideshare Program.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M Series A at $1.1B Valuation
summary: Starcloud has raised $170 million in a Series A funding round, achieving a $1.1 billion valuation. The investment underscores strong investor interest in orbital data center ventures, with Starcloud standing out for its flight heritage.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate to develop a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing to safely remove satellites from orbit.

title: In-Orbit Logistics Companies Call for Clearer Demand Signals from Gov
summary: In-orbit logistics companies urge the government to provide clearer demand signals. The Space Development Agency highlights the challenges of operating in space.

title: Canada’s Northwestel to Provide Broadband With Telesat Lightspeed Capacity
summary: Canada’s Northwestel has signed a multi-year contract with Telesat to use capacity from its Lightspeed constellation for broadband services. This partnership will help Northwestel expand broadband access in Canada’s northern regions, including Nunavut and the Northwest Territories.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Component Supplier Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space Corporation has acquired Thermal Management Technologies (TMT) to support its orbital data center constellation development and vertical integration strategy.

title: ArkEdge Space to Work With ABIT For IoT Service Rollout
summary: ArkEdge Space and ABIT Corporation signed an MoU to jointly develop IoT solutions and satellite communication devices. The partnership aims to advance satellite-based IoT services.

title: NASA Launches Artemis II in Further Step Toward a Moon Return
summary: NASA launched Artemis II, its first crewed mission under the Artemis program, to test the Orion spacecraft's life support systems with live astronauts. This mission marks a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: CanarySat CEO Antonio Abad Outlines the Sovereign, Secure Approach Behind the Magec Constellation
summary: CanarySat, backed by Arquimea, unveiled its Magec constellation, a secure Ka-band LEO satellite network, during SATShow Week. The constellation emphasizes sovereign and secure satellite services from Spain's Canary Islands.

title: Bloomberg, CNBC Report SpaceX Submitted Confidential Filing for IPO
summary: SpaceX has confidentially filed for an initial public offering (IPO) according to reports from Bloomberg and CNBC. A confidential filing allows the company to submit a draft IPO registration to the SEC.

title: Measat Appoints New CTO
summary: Measat Global appointed John Loke as its new Chief Technology Officer to lead the company's digital evolution and regional strategy. Loke will oversee technological advancements and strategic initiatives for the satellite operator.

title: Live in the Booth: Arianespace CEO David Cavaillolès Previews Ariane 6 Ramp-Up
summary: Arianespace is progressing with its launch campaign for Amazon's Leo satellites, following the first successful launch for its largest customer in February. The next mission is scheduled for April.
